title: श्री सिद्धिविनायक स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सिद्धिविनायक स्तोत्रम् ॥

जय उत्तमं श्रीगणनायकं प्रभुं
सुराधिनाथं सकलार्थदायकम्।
नमामि सिद्धिप्रदसिद्धिविघ्नपं
तमेकदन्तं शिवतेजसम्भवम्॥१॥

विनायकं विघ्नहरं गणेशं
हेरम्बमीडे प्रथमं सुरेशम्।
सिद्धिप्रदं सिद्धिविनायकं तं
बुद्धिप्रदं भक्तवरं नमामि॥२॥

गजाननं गौरीसुतं गणेशं
शिवात्मजं शंकरचित्तरञ्जनम्।
स्कन्दाग्रजं सर्वगुणैकधामं
सिद्धिप्रदं सिद्धिविनायकं भजे॥३॥

महोदरं मूषकवाहनं प्रभुं
पाशाङ्कुशाभीतिवरान्वितं करैः।
मोदाकहस्तं हसितप्रसन्नं
सिद्धिप्रदं सिद्धिविनायकं भजे॥४॥

मङ्गलमूर्तिं वरसिद्धिदं प्रभुं
लम्बोदरं चन्द्रकलावतंसकम्।
विघ्नेश्वरं विघ्नहरं सुरेशं
सिद्धिप्रदं सिद्धिविनायकं भजे॥५॥

वक्रतुण्ड महाकाय सूर्यकोटिसमप्रभ।
निर्विघ्नं कुरु मे देव सर्वकार्येषु सर्वदा॥६॥

सिद्धिबुद्धिसमेतं वन्दे विघ्नेश्वरं गणम्।
पार्वतीनन्दनं देवं लोकवन्दितपादुकम्॥७॥

गणाधिपाय विद्महे वक्रतुण्डाय धीमहि।
तन्नो दन्ती प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ इति श्रीसिद्धिविनायक स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Siddhi Vinayak Stotram ||

Jaya Uttamam Shrigananayakam Prabhum
Suradhinatham Sakalarthadayakam.
Namami Siddhipradasiddhivighnapam
Tamekadantam Shivatejasambhavam.||1||

Vinayakam Vighnaharam Ganesham
Herambameede Prathamam Suresham.
Siddhipradam Siddhivinayakam Tam
Buddhipradam Bhaktavaram Namami.||2||

Gajananam Gaurisutam Ganesham
Shivaatmajam Shankarachittaranjanam.
Skandaagrajam Sarvagunaaikadhamam
Siddhipradam Siddhivinayakam Bhaje.||3||

Mahodaram Mushakavaahanam Prabhum
Pashankushabheetivaraanvitam Karaih.
Modakahastam Hasitaprasannam
Siddhipradam Siddhivinayakam Bhaje.||4||

Mangalamurtim Varasiddhidam Prabhum
Lambodaram Chandrakalaavatasakam.
Vighnesvaram Vighnaharam Suresham
Siddhipradam Siddhivinayakam Bhaje.||5||

Vakratunda Mahakaya Suryakotisamaprabha.
Nirvighnam Kuru Me Deva Sarvakaryeshu Sarvada.||6||

Siddhibuddhisametam Vande Vighnesvaram Ganam.
Parvatinanadanam Devam Lokavanditapadukam.||7||

Ganadhipaya Vidmahe Vakratundaya Dheemahi.
Tanno Danti Prachodayaat.

|| Iti Shri Siddhi Vinayak Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
(1) गणों के नायक, देवों के अधिनाथ, सम्पूर्ण अर्थों को देने वाले, सिद्धि और विघ्ननाश के प्रदाता, एकदन्त, शिव के तेज से उत्पन्न उत्तम प्रभु की मैं जय करता हूँ और नमन करता हूँ।

(2) विनायक, विघ्नहर, गणेश, हेरम्ब, देवों में प्रथम पूज्य, सिद्धि देने वाले, सिद्धिविनायक, बुद्धि प्रदान करने वाले, भक्तों में श्रेष्ठ को मैं नमस्कार करता हूँ।

(3) गजानन, गौरी के पुत्र, शिव के आत्मज, शंकर के चित्त को रंजित करने वाले, स्कन्द (कार्तिकेय) के अग्रज, सर्वगुणों के एकमात्र धाम, सिद्धिप्रद सिद्धिविनायक को मैं भजता हूँ।

(4) विशाल उदर वाले, मूषक वाहन वाले, हाथों में पाश, अंकुश, अभय और वर मुद्रा धारण करने वाले, मोदक हाथ में लिए हुए, हँसते हुए प्रसन्नमुख सिद्धिविनायक को मैं भजता हूँ।

(5) मंगलमूर्ति, श्रेष्ठ सिद्धि देने वाले, लम्बोदर, चन्द्रकला का मुकुट धारण करने वाले, विघ्नेश्वर, विघ्नहर, देवों के ईश्वर सिद्धिविनायक को मैं भजता हूँ।

(6) हे वक्रतुण्ड! हे महाकाय! करोड़ों सूर्य के समान प्रभा वाले! हे देव! मेरे सभी कार्यों में सदा निर्विघ्नता प्रदान करें।

(7) सिद्धि और बुद्धि से युक्त विघ्नेश्वर गण को, पार्वती के पुत्र को, जिनकी पादुकाओं को सम्पूर्ण लोक वन्दन करता है, मैं नमन करता हूँ।
benefits: Invokes Siddhi Vinayak form of Ganesha for guaranteed success
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles from new ventures, business, and studies
Includes the Ganesha Gayatri mantra for deep spiritual benefit
Traditionally associated with auspiciousness (mangal) into all aspects of life
Especially powerful when recited before important undertakings
description: The Siddhi Vinayak Stotram, known in Sanskrit as श्री सिद्धिविनायक स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Ganesha. Rooted in Puranic Compilation, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to invoke Siddhi Vinayak form of Ganesha for guaranteed success as well as include the Ganesha Gayatri mantra for deep spiritual benefit. Additionally, it is known to grant both Siddhi (accomplishment) and Buddhi (wisdom). This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Ganesh Chaturthi to invoke divine grace. At 7 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Siddhi Vinayak Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Siddhi Vinayak Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to Ganesha as Siddhi Vinayak for guaranteed success, wisdom, and removal of all obstacles.
relatedSearches: Siddhi Vinayak Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit

Siddhi Vinayak Mahima eight verse hymn

seoFaqs.question: What does the title 'Siddhi Vinayak' mean and how is it iconographically distinct?
seoFaqs.answer: Siddhi Vinayaka literally means 'Vinayaka who confers siddhi (success, accomplishment, supernatural attainment)'. Iconographically the form is distinguished by the trunk curving to the right, called dakshina-mukha or valampuri. Hindu iconographic manuals declare that a right-curving trunk indicates rapid and absolute fulfilment of devotee wishes, but also a stricter, more demanding form of worship requiring exact ritual purity. By contrast, the more common left-curving trunk (vama-mukha) is held to be milder and easier to propitiate. The Siddhi Vinayak Stotram is therefore framed for serious sankalpa-driven worship and includes verses requesting accuracy of ritual and forgiveness of any inadvertent error. (Sources: Wikipedia Siddhivinayak Mumbai; Holidify.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Siddhi Vinayak Stotram and the Mumbai Prabhadevi temple?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shree Siddhivinayak Ganapati Mandir at Prabhadevi in Mumbai, originally consecrated on 19 November 1801 by Laxman Vithu and Deubai Patil, is the most popular contemporary shrine for the Siddhi Vinayaka form. Its sanctum houses a self-manifest 2.5-foot black-stone murti with the right-curving trunk and four arms holding lotus, axe, rosary and modaka, flanked by Riddhi and Siddhi. The temple's daily liturgy includes recitation of Sankat Nashan Ganesh Stotra, Atharvashirsha and the Siddhi Vinayak Stotram, with Tuesday seeing the most footfall as Mangalwar is sacred to Ganesha. The Mumbai temple has effectively standardised the modern recital of the Stotram across Maharashtra and globally. (Sources: Wikipedia Siddhivinayak Mumbai; Mumbai City government; Siddhivinayak.org.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Siddhatek Ashtavinayak temple relate to Siddhi Vinayak worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Siddhatek, on an island in the Bhima river in Ahmadnagar district of Maharashtra, is the second of the eight Ashtavinayak temples and is the only one whose murti has the trunk curving to the right, qualifying it as the original Siddhi Vinayaka in the Ashtavinayak cycle. The temple is traditionally credited with being the place where Vishnu propitiated Siddhi Vinayaka before his battle with the demons Madhu and Kaitabha, as recounted in the Mudgala Purana. The Siddhi Vinayak Stotram is recited at Siddhatek as a pilgrimage closing prayer, and many Maharashtrian families consider darshan at Siddhatek essential before approaching the Mumbai Prabhadevi temple. (Sources: Wikipedia Siddhivinayak Siddhatek; Holidify.)

seoFaqs.question: Where in the Mudgala Purana is the Siddhi Vinayak narrative preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mudgala Purana, devoted exclusively to Ganesha and his eight principal avataras, places the Siddhi Vinayaka episode within its account of Vishnu's pre-cosmic preparations. As Vishnu sleeps on the cosmic ocean in yoga-nidra, Brahma emerges from his navel-lotus and begins creation; the demons Madhu and Kaitabha emerge from Vishnu's ear and threaten Brahma. Vishnu, awakened, cannot prevail against them until he propitiates Siddhi Vinayaka, after which he attains the siddhi to slay them. This narrative establishes Siddhi Vinayaka as the deity whom even Vishnu must invoke before any difficult undertaking, theologically grounding the Stotram's claim of universal efficacy. (Sources: Wikipedia Siddhivinayak Mumbai citing Mudgala Purana; Siddhatek lore.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the recommended day and time to recite Siddhi Vinayak Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday is the principal day, since Mangalwar is sacred to Ganesha in many Maharashtrian and Konkani traditions (in addition to the standard Wednesday in some North Indian schools). Within Tuesday, brahma-muhurta and the noon abhishek-time at Prabhadevi are most auspicious. Annually, Angarki Sankashti Chaturthi (when Sankashti falls on Tuesday) is the supreme day, attracting over 100,000 devotees to Prabhadevi. Other key dates include Ganesh Chaturthi in Bhadrapada, Ganesh Jayanti in Magha, the temple's foundation anniversary on Kartik Shukla Chaturthi, and any personal sankalpa day for new business, real-estate transactions or marriage proposals. (Sources: Yatra; Holidify.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Siddhi Vinayak Stotram differ from the more general Ganesha Sankat Nashan Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Sankat Nashan Ganesh Stotra (from the Narada Purana) is a broad obstacle-removal prayer addressed to Ganesha generically with twelve names. Siddhi Vinayak Stotram, by contrast, is specifically addressed to the Siddhi Vinayaka form (right-trunked, accompanied by Riddhi and Siddhi as consorts) and is oriented toward the active conferral of siddhi (accomplishment) rather than merely the removal of obstacles. The Sankat Nashan is reactive (clearing what blocks); the Siddhi Vinayak is proactive (establishing what one seeks). Both are commonly chanted together in major sankalpa pujas, with the Sankat Nashan first to clear the field and the Siddhi Vinayak second to crystallise the goal. (Sources: Mahakatha Siddhi Vinayaka mantra; Wikipedia Siddhivinayak.)
